perturbation.original_text: f. “Potential Customer” shall mean any Person that Employee directly solicited, targeted or specifically identified as a prospective or potential customer, or about which Employee obtained information on behalf of a Banking Organization for purposes of directly soliciting, targeting or specifically identifying as a prospective or potential customer, during the one (1) year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution.
perturbation.changed_text: f. “Potential Customer” shall mean any Person that Employee directly solicited, targeted or specifically identified as a prospective or potential customer, or about which Employee obtained information on behalf of a Banking Organization for purposes of directly soliciting, targeting or specifically identifying as a prospective or potential customer, during the one (1) year period prior to Employee’s termination of employment with either Financial Institution. However, a potential customer shall only be deemed as such, if the banking organization has extended an official offer for Banking and Financial Services to the potential customer.
perturbation.explanation: The added sentence to the definition of "Potential Customer" introduces an in-text contradiction. Initially, the definition includes anyone the Employee solicited or targeted within a year. The new sentence adds that the Banking Organization must have extended an 'official offer' to that potential customer, contradicting the initial broad definition in Section 3. This creates ambiguity when applying the non-solicitation agreements in Section 5 because it's uncertain which individuals qualify as 'Potential Customers', and hence, are protected by the non-solicitation agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers. Employee acknowledges and agrees that by virtue of Employee’s position with a Banking Organization, Employee has or will have advantageous familiarity and personal contacts with Customers and Potential Customers and will have advantageous familiarity with the business operations and affairs of a Banking Organization. Employee further acknowledges and agrees that Employee has been or will be placed in a position that, were Employee to leave a Financial Institution to compete with the business of a Banking Organization, such entity from which the Employee departed would, upon violation of this Agreement, suffer irreparable harm and financial loss, as well as lost goodwill and Confidential Information. Therefore, in consideration of the covenants of all Financial Institutions contained herein, Employee agrees that during Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution, and for a period of one (1) year immediately following the termination of Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution (“Restriction Period”), for whatever reason and regardless of how Employee’s employment was terminated, Employee shall not, directly or indirectly (including, but not limited to, through other individuals, any partnership, corporation or business entity in which Employee has an ownership interest or serves as an officer, employee, independent contractor, representative, agent or consultant), either for Employee’s own benefit or the benefit of any other Person: a. Solicit, divert, or take away (or attempt to Solicit, divert or take away) any Banking and Financial Services to or from any Customer or Potential Customer; or b. advise, persuade or induce (or attempt to advise, persuade or induce) any Customer or Potential Customer to terminate, reduce, limit, or change the Customer’s services or business relationship, or the Potential Customer’s contemplated services or business relationship, with a Financial Institution; or c. directly provide Banking and Financial Services to, or directly accept Banking and Financial Services from, any Customer or Potential Customer. This Agreement is not intended to prevent Employee from accepting employment with an organization that competes with the Banking Organizations by offering Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers. The Parties acknowledge that Employee may be associated with such an Organization and that such association may place Employee in a senior or supervisory role to those in the organization that have contact with Customers or Potential Customers. Employee shall not be in violation of this Agreement merely by engaging in such a role, which may include: a. supervising those who provide Banking and Financial Services to Customers or Potential Customers; b. engaging in credit review, underwriting, loan approval, or financial analysis of a Customer or Potential Customer’s business as a part of a supervisory or management role, so long as such activity does not require the disclosure of Confidential Information, direct contact with the Customer or Potential Customer, or the disclosure of Employee’s role to the Customer or Potential Customer; c. performing or supervising those that perform data processing, accounting, rate review, document review or similar “back room” services related to a Customer or Potential Customer so long as the services do not require the disclosure of Confidential Information or contact with the Customer or Potential Customer.
perturbation.justification: The change to the definition of "Potential Customer" adds a requirement that an official offer must be extended, which contradicts the original broader definition that included anyone the employee solicited or targeted. This new requirement conflicts with Section 5, which restricts solicitation of 'Potential Customers' based on the original, broader definition. This creates a strong in-text contradiction because it is now unclear which 'Potential Customers' are subject to the non-solicitation agreement in Section 5.

perturbation.original_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6, herein, except that any claim for damages shall be capped at $1,000.
perturbation.explanation: This modification in Section 9 introduces a contradiction. The original text states that breaches of Sections 4, 5, or 6 will cause irreparable damage, suggesting that monetary damages are insufficient. The added clause then places a cap on monetary damages ($1,000), creating an in-text contradiction. If the damage is irreparable and no adequate remedy at law exists, limiting monetary recovery conflicts with the irreparable harm assertion. This can affect the enforceability of the agreement and the remedies available to the Banking Organization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Available Relief . Employee agrees that a Banking Organization, or its successor or assigns, will suffer irreparable damage and injury and will not have an adequate remedy at law in the event of any breach by Employee of any provision of Sections 4, 5 or 6 , herein. Accordingly, in the event of a breach or of a threatened or attempted breach by Employee of Sections 4, 5, or 6, in addition to all other remedies to which a Banking Organization is entitled under law, in equity, or otherwise (including, but not limited to, monetary damages), a Banking Organization or its assigns and successors, shall be entitled to a temporary restraining order and/or preliminary or permanent injunction (without the necessity of showing any actual damage) or a decree of specific performance and no bond or other security shall be required in that connection.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that a breach of sections 4, 5, or 6 will cause irreparable damage and that no adequate remedy at law exists. The changed text introduces a cap of $1,000 on monetary damages for such breaches. This directly contradicts the assertion in both the changed text and the contradicted text, which states that monetary damages are insufficient to compensate for the harm caused by a breach, as it limits the available remedy to a fixed amount. This creates a logical inconsistency as $1000 is not a reasonable remedy for 'irreparable damage and injury' and it contradicts the statement that 'no adequate remedy at law' exists.

perturbation.original_text: The covenants set forth in this Agreement are essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as independent of any other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization.
perturbation.changed_text: The covenants set forth in this Agreement are essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as independent of any other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization, except that the enforceability of Section 5 is contingent upon the employee receiving a positive performance review within the six months preceding termination.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in Section 14 states that the covenants are 'independent' of other obligations. However, the added clause makes the enforceability of Section 5 (Non-Solicitation of Customers and Potential Customers) 'contingent' upon a positive performance review. This introduces an in-text contradiction: the covenants are both independent and contingent on another condition. This creates uncertainty about whether the non-solicitation clause is fully enforceable if the employee's performance review is not positive, which undermines its initial purpose of protecting the Banking Organization.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14. “No-Defense” Provision . The covenants set forth in this Agreement are essential terms and conditions to a Banking Organization employing or continuing to employ Employee, and shall be construed as independent of any other obligations or agreements between Employee and a Banking Organization. The existence of any claim or cause of action Employee may have against a Banking Organization, including, but not limited to, the alleged material breach by a Banking Organization of any agreement with Employee, shall not constitute a defense to the enforcement by a Banking Organization of the covenants and obligations in this Agreement and shall not relieve Employee of his or her obligations under this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies Section 14, introducing a contingency for the enforceability of Section 5 (Non-Solicitation) based on performance reviews. The original text in Section 14, which is also the contradicted text, explicitly states that the covenants are 'independent' of other obligations. The added clause directly contradicts this independence by making the enforceability of a specific covenant contingent on a performance review, creating a clear inconsistency within the document.

perturbation.original_text: Employee represents and warrants that he/she is not a party to an agreement with his or her prior employer(s) (other than Heritage or HopFed) or any other Person that prohibits Solicitation of business or competing with that employer or other Person.
perturbation.changed_text: Employee represents and warrants that he/she is not a party to an agreement with his or her prior employer(s) (other than Heritage or HopFed) or any other Person that prohibits Solicitation of business or competing with that employer or other Person. However, this representation does not apply to non-binding ethical guidelines or aspirational codes of conduct previously agreed upon by the employee.
perturbation.explanation: The initial statement is an absolute representation. Adding the exception creates ambiguity and therefore a contradiction. If the original statement says that the employee is not subject to any agreement, and the new addition is stating that non-binding ethical guidelines is not considered to be part of that agreement. The original intent is being compromised and therefore is creating an in-text contradiction
perturbation.contradicted_text: 22. Representations. Employee represents and warrants that he/she is not a party to an agreement with his or her prior employer(s) (other than Heritage or HopFed) or any other Person that prohibits Solicitation of business or competing with that employer or other Person. Employee affirms and represents that performance of his/her job duties and responsibilities for a Financial Institution does not, and will not, breach any agreement or covenant not to Solicit business, compete in business, or keep in confidence proprietary or confidential information acquired in confidence or in trust prior to Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution. Employee further represents that he/she has not retained any documents or information relating to Employee’s prior employers (other than Heritage or HopFed) that may be considered confidential or proprietary information and that Employee has not disclosed or used, and will not disclose or use, any information relating to his or her prior employer(s) in connection with Employee’s employment with a Financial Institution.
perturbation.justification: The original text states an absolute representation that the employee is not a party to any agreement prohibiting solicitation or competition. The changed text introduces an exception for non-binding ethical guidelines. The contradicted text reinforces the absolute representation in the original text. By introducing an exception, the changed text creates a contradiction as it allows for a scenario where the employee *is* subject to some form of agreement, even if non-binding, which the contradicted text states is not the case.
